description: In 2022, The Walt Disney Company faced a backlash from employees and conservative politicians after initially remaining silent on Florida's 'Parental Rights in Education' bill. The situation escalated into a full-scale corporate-state conflict involving the revocation of Disney's special tax district status in Florida.

key_events.description: Disney faces internal employee walkouts for failing to oppose the bill.
CEO Bob Chapek formally denounces the law, triggers retaliation from Florida Governor.
Florida legislature moves to dissolve Disney's Reedy Creek Improvement District.
Disney files federal lawsuit alleging retaliation, cementing a polarized public stance.

calibration_notes: Excellent for measuring the impact of 'corporate activism' in an increasingly hyper-polarized political environment.
